summary: The diagnosis of severe preeclampsia requires proteinuria exceeding 300mg/24 hours according to multiple authoritative medical guidelines including ACOG standards. This criterion has broad acceptance across different reputable studies and professional organizations. To clarify your options further would be redundant as it matches exactly what was previously determined based on these reliable resources. Therefore, no change needs to be made regarding selection C which reflects this specific diagnostic marker accurately.
